No Poverty.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly mentions 'Real option valuation for pharma. R&D investment and licensing,' directly supporting Target 3.b on research and development of medicines.
evidence: Real option valuation for pharma. R&D investment and licensing

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education and skills for employment in accounting and valuation, directly aligning with Targets 4.3 and 4.4.
evidence: help the NCCU accounting students pursue valuation knowledge
develop the student's ability to report on and analyze value of intangible assets

Gender Equality.reason: The general mention of 'social responsibility' is too broad to infer a direct link to gender equality without specific textual support.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course's focus on valuation for transactions (IPOs, M&A) and creating value directly supports economic growth and productive activities (Targets 8.2, 8.3).
evidence: Valuation by itself is a profession
duty of care to manage and create value

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course title 'Reporting and Valuation of Intangible Assets' and mentions of 'technology transfer' and 'R&D investment' directly align with innovation and technology development (Targets 9.5, 9.b).
evidence: Reporting and Valuation of Intangible Assets
Real option valuation for pharma. R&D investment

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course covers international financial standards (IFRS), which can indirectly contribute to better regulation and monitoring of global financial markets, supporting Target 10.5.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: A core competency is to 'identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' which directly links to Target 12.6 on integrating sustainability into company reporting.
evidence: understand and be able to identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues
Reporting and Valuation of Intangible Assets

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on 'laws, accounting pronouncements, valuation standards,' 'litigation,' and 'ethics' directly supports developing accountable institutions and reducing corruption (Targets 16.5, 16.6).
evidence: discussion of pertinent laws, accounting pronouncements, and valuation standards
identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course description or objectives links to global partnerships for sustainable development.
